task_completion.description: Navigate to the TailwindTraders project backlog
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TailwindTraders project backlog or boards view is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Apply the 'security' tag to relevant work items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'security' tag is visible on work items related to authentication, vulnerabilities, or rate limiting.

task_completion.description: Apply the 'performance' tag to relevant work items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'performance' tag is visible on work items related to speed, indexing, or resource exhaustion.

task_completion.description: Apply the 'api' tag to relevant work items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'api' tag is visible on work items related to endpoints, REST, or backend services.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Cross-Cutting Concerns' folder under Shared Queries
task_completion.visual_evidence: A folder named 'Cross-Cutting Concerns' is visible under the 'Shared Queries' section in the Queries interface.

task_completion.description: Create the three required shared queries inside the new folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Queries named 'Security Items', 'Performance Items', and 'API Items' are visible inside the 'Cross-Cutting Concerns' folder.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the web interface to apply tags and create queries
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser interface for the project management tool being used, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Items purely about UI/frontend should not be tagged with cross-cutting tags
integrity.visual_evidence: Work items that are clearly only about UI/frontend do not have 'security', 'performance', or 'api' tags.

integrity.description: Queries are genuinely created in the system
integrity.visual_evidence: The queries appear in the actual project management UI's query list, not just typed in a text editor or search bar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project Name: TailwindTraders. Expected Folder: Cross-Cutting Concerns. Expected Queries: Security Items, Performance Items, API Items. Note: Some expected work items might not exist in the environment due to a known defect, but verify any visible tags and the creation of the folder and queries based on what is available.
